overrides.override_summary: California Fast Food Act (AB 1228) compliance is franchisee's sole responsibility. No training, assistance, or fees from McDonald's.
overrides.why_it_matters: California franchisees face higher labor costs due to state-mandated minimum wage and working condition standards. The franchisor provides no support.

overrides.override_summary: No signed statement, questionnaire, or acknowledgment may waive claims under applicable state franchise law (including fraud in the inducement) or disclaim reliance on franchisor statements.
overrides.why_it_matters: Preserves franchisee's right to pursue franchise law claims regardless of contract acknowledgments. Sections 28(b),(c),(d),(i) of FA are deleted for Hawaii.

overrides.override_summary: Any release required for renewal or transfer does not apply to liability under Maryland Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law.
overrides.why_it_matters: Franchisee cannot be forced to release Maryland franchise law claims as a condition of renewal or transfer.

overrides.override_summary: Termination upon insolvency might not be enforceable under federal bankruptcy law (11 USC §101 et seq.).
overrides.why_it_matters: Bankruptcy filing may not automatically trigger franchise termination in Maryland.

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may bring claims arising under MD Franchise Law in Maryland, subject to any arbitration obligations.
overrides.why_it_matters: Overrides the Illinois forum selection for Maryland franchise law claims.

overrides.override_summary: Maryland law may apply to MD Franchise Law claims, subject to arbitration obligation.
overrides.why_it_matters: Supplements Illinois governing law for state-specific claims.

overrides.override_summary: MD Franchise Law claims must be brought within 3 years after the grant of the franchise.
overrides.why_it_matters: Sets a specific limitations period for Maryland claims.

overrides.override_summary: McDonald's protects franchisees against claims of infringement or unfair competition arising from authorized mark use.
overrides.why_it_matters: Explicit trademark defense commitment for Minnesota franchisees.

overrides.override_summary: 90 days notice of termination (60 days to cure); 180 days notice of non-renewal. Transfer consent not unreasonably withheld. Nothing abrogates Minn. Stat. Ch. 80C.
overrides.why_it_matters: Significantly stronger termination protection. Cure period and advance notice requirements exceed base franchise agreement.

overrides.override_summary: Post-term noncompete covenants generally considered unenforceable in North Dakota.
overrides.why_it_matters: The 18-month/10-mile noncompete is likely void in North Dakota.

overrides.override_summary: North Dakota law governs claims arising under the North Dakota Franchise Investment Law.
overrides.why_it_matters: Overrides Illinois governing law for ND franchise law claims.

overrides.override_summary: If WA Franchise Investment Protection Act (RCW §19.100.180) applies, its provisions prevail over inconsistent franchise agreement terms.
overrides.why_it_matters: Washington state law overrides conflicting franchise agreement provisions.

overrides.override_summary: Assurance of Discontinuance with WA AG: removed no-poach/no-solicitation provision from franchise agreements. Agreed not to enforce in existing agreements. Requested amendment by franchisees. No fines assessed.
overrides.why_it_matters: Major antitrust concession. Franchise employees in Washington are free to work at other McDonald's locations.
